summary: Device Health Rule Audit Logging feature testing complete. All 22 backend API tests passed (100%). Audit logging for PUT (change_type='update') and PATCH toggle (change_type='toggle') is fully functional. All required features verified: audit entries contain full config snapshots (old_config, new_config), changed_by_name shows operator email, ip_address captured, created_at timestamp, descending order, RBAC enforcement (operator/admin only), no internal IDs leaked.
critical_code_review_comments: PUT /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name} creates audit entry with change_type='update' - WORKING
PATCH /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/toggle creates audit entry with change_type='toggle' - WORKING
old_config captures state BEFORE mutation - VERIFIED
new_config captures state AFTER mutation - VERIFIED
Both configs contain full snapshots: enabled, threshold_json, cooldown_minutes, severity - VERIFIED
GET /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/audit-log returns entries in descending order - VERIFIED
GET audit-log with guardian token returns 403 - VERIFIED
GET audit-log for fake_rule returns 404 - VERIFIED
No 'id' or 'changed_by' UUID fields in response - VERIFIED
Atomicity: PUT/PATCH success implies audit entry exists - VERIFIED
Multiple operations create sequential audit entries - VERIFIED
Audit log works for all 3 rules: low_battery, signal_degradation, reboot_anomaly - VERIFIED
seed_data_creation: None - tests use existing rules. All rules restored to original values after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Audit logging feature for device health rules is fully functional. Endpoint: GET /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/audit-log. Response fields: rule_name, changed_by_name, change_type, old_config, new_config, ip_address, created_at. No 'id' or 'changed_by' fields exposed. change_type is 'update' for PUT and 'toggle' for PATCH toggle. Both old_config and new_config contain full snapshots with keys: enabled, threshold_json, cooldown_minutes, severity.
verified_features: PUT /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name} creates an audit log entry with change_type='update' - PASS
PATCH /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/toggle creates an audit log entry with change_type='toggle' - PASS
Audit log entry contains: rule_name, changed_by_name, change_type, old_config, new_config, ip_address, created_at - PASS
old_config and new_config are full snapshots (enabled, threshold_json, cooldown_minutes, severity) - PASS
old_config reflects the state BEFORE the mutation - PASS
new_config reflects the state AFTER the mutation - PASS
GET /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/audit-log returns audit entries in descending order (most recent first) - PASS
GET /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/audit-log with guardian token returns 403 - PASS
Audit log entry has no internal IDs (no 'id' or 'changed_by' UUID fields) - PASS
changed_by_name shows operator email or full_name - PASS
After multiple PUT/PATCH operations, audit log shows all changes in order - PASS
Atomicity: if the endpoint returns success, the audit entry exists; both are committed together - PASS
